title: senna sulfurea
intro: Senna sulfurea, also known as yellow cassia, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a laxative and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant contains compounds like sennosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help to relieve constipation, as well as flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Senna sulfurea can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
